expectations.text: Recommends IRSA or Assume Role method (not just Access Key method)
expectations.evidence: "Infisical server connects to your AWS account using a long-lived IAM user or role...Dynamic Secret Engine generates temporary credentials on-demand using AWS STS"

expectations.text: Correctly identifies Temporary Credentials type (not IAM User type) for short-lived STS creds
expectations.evidence: Does not explicitly identify 'Temporary Credentials' as the correct type vs IAM User type. Discusses STS but doesn't clearly differentiate credential types

expectations.text: Notes the 1-hour maximum TTL for AssumeRole with temporary credentials
expectations.evidence: Mentions '1-hour TTL' in configuration but does not explain this as the AssumeRole maximum limitation

expectations.text: Shows correct IAM permissions policy needed for STS operations
expectations.evidence: Shows IAM policy with 'sts:AssumeRole' and creates role with STS trust relationship

expectations.text: Mentions KUBERNETES_AUTO_FETCH_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN env var for IRSA setup
Explains that lease returns include ACCESS_KEY, SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, and SESSION_TOKEN
